mapping.a_element: Metropolis Monte Carlo simulation of a 3D lattice spin model
mapping.b_element: Monte Carlo simulation of the 3D one-component phi^4 lattice model

mapping.a_element: finite-size (size-dependence) scaling analysis of the observable
critical point T=T_c of a 3D Ising-type system
mapping.b_element: critical point of the 3D phi^4 (Ising universality) system

brief_justification: The overlap is only the shared field toolkit (Monte Carlo + finite-size scaling of a 3D Ising-type critical point), which is generic to lattice statistical physics, not distinctive transferable machinery. The distinctive mechanism each paper is actually about differs: paper A studies relaxational (model-A) critical DYNAMICS to extract the dynamic exponent z=2.35 from autocorrelation times, whereas paper B is a purely STATIC analysis of corrections to scaling (tuning the coupling lambda to a leading-correction-free improved model) to extract the correction exponent omega; z and omega play different roles, and the diluted-Ising vs pure-Ising universality classes differ, so no role-for-role mapping of the core content holds.
